title: Meeting Lukas
desc: Introduce yourself to a new acquaintance
setting: On the train, a friendly young man strikes up a conversation.

options.feedback: Great — 'ich chume us...' is exactly how to say where you're from.
He'll understand, but a full sentence sounds friendlier. Return his 'Sali' too.
Honest if you're lost — but this one you can handle. 'Ich chume us...' is the answer.

steps.speaker: Lukas
steps.en: Ah, nice! And what's your name?

options.en: My name is Tom. And you?
options.feedback: Perfect — answering and turning the question back keeps the chat flowing.
Fine, though 'ich heisse Tom, und du?' feels warmer and keeps it going.
That comes off suspicious — he's just being friendly. Swiss small talk is gentle; meet it.

steps.speaker: Lukas
steps.es: Ich bi de Lukas. Wohnsch jetz da z'Luzärn?
steps.en: I'm Lukas. Do you live here in Lucerne now?

options.es: Ja, ich bi grad iizoge. Es gfallt mer.
options.en: Yes, I just moved in. I like it.
options.feedback: Lovely — sharing that you like it invites a warm response. Natural and open.
Clear and correct. A little detail would make it warmer, but this is solid.
Far too cold for friendly small talk! He's being kind — keep it open.

steps.speaker: Lukas
steps.es: Schön di z'kenne! Viel Glück z'Luzärn.
steps.en: Nice to meet you! Good luck in Lucerne.

options.feedback: Warm and natural — using his name and 'merci glich' is a perfect sign-off.
Friendly and easy. 'Dir au' (you too) is just right.
A bit flat after such a friendly chat. A 'merci' and 'tschüss' would land better.
